_comment: GROUND TRUTH RCA LABELS — This is what the GNN learns to predict
_source: Derived from trouble ticket INC00847291, validated by senior transport engineer
_confidence: HIGH — confirmed by physical SFP replacement resolving all symptoms

node_labels._comment: Per-node classification labels for GNN node classification task

_label_schema.root_cause: The originating faulty element — where the fix should be applied
_label_schema.primary_symptom: Directly connected to root cause, first-order effect
_label_schema.secondary_symptom: Affected by cascading propagation, second+ order effect
_label_schema.collateral: Not directly in fault path but impacted by traffic rerouting or overload

gNB-NYC-E-047.label: primary_symptom
gNB-NYC-E-047.reasoning: Directly served by the failed backhaul path. Lost S1/NG connectivity, all 3 sectors went unavailable. First RAN element to show symptoms.

gNB-NYC-E-048.label: collateral
gNB-NYC-E-048.reasoning: Connected to same AGG-NYC-E-01 via CSG-NYC-E-02 (different link). Experienced throughput degradation due to backhaul congestion from rerouted traffic, not direct fault path.
gNB-NYC-E-048.impact: Throughput degraded 50%, handover failures to site 047

gNB-NYC-E-049.label: unaffected
gNB-NYC-E-049.reasoning: Connected to AGG-NYC-E-02 (different aggregation router). No direct or indirect impact from the SFP failure. Normal KPIs throughout.

eNB-NYC-E-047.label: primary_symptom
eNB-NYC-E-047.reasoning: Co-located with gNB-NYC-E-047, shares same CSG-NYC-E-01 backhaul. Lost S1 connectivity to core. All 3 LTE sectors unavailable.

CSG-NYC-E-01.label: primary_symptom
CSG-NYC-E-01.reasoning: Directly connected to root cause element via the failed link E005. Lost OSPF adjacency, MPLS LSP down. All traffic from site 047 lost upstream path.
CSG-NYC-E-01.impact: Complete loss of upstream connectivity for site 047

CSG-NYC-E-02.label: collateral
CSG-NYC-E-02.reasoning: Connected to same AGG-NYC-E-01 but via different interface (Gi0/0/0/4 — healthy). Experienced congestion from rerouted traffic, not from the fault itself.
CSG-NYC-E-02.impact: High utilization warning from rerouted traffic

CSG-NYC-E-03.label: unaffected
CSG-NYC-E-03.reasoning: Connected to AGG-NYC-E-02, completely separate fault domain. No impact.

AGG-NYC-E-01.label: root_cause
AGG-NYC-E-01.reasoning: Faulty SFP transceiver on interface Gi0/0/0/3 caused CRC errors → link flapping → link failure. This is the originating fault element. Replacing the SFP resolved all 47 alarms.
AGG-NYC-E-01.impact: Source of fault — cascaded to 9 other network elements

root_cause_detail.fault_mechanism: SFP optical power degradation leading to CRC errors, link flapping, and eventual link failure
contributing_factors: Ambient temperature: 42.5°C (within spec but accelerates aging)

remediation.detail: Replaced SFP-10G-LR module in slot Gi0/0/0/3

AGG-NYC-E-02.label: collateral
AGG-NYC-E-02.reasoning: Not in the direct fault path but received rerouted traffic via inter-AGG link E008. Experienced increased utilization.

CORE-RTR-NYC-01.label: secondary_symptom
CORE-RTR-NYC-01.reasoning: Two hops from root cause. Experienced increased OSPF SPF calculations and elevated utilization from rerouted traffic and reconvergence events.

UPF-NYC-01.label: secondary_symptom
UPF-NYC-01.reasoning: Core network function affected by loss of N3 path to gNB-NYC-E-047. Session drops and QoS violations are downstream effects of the backhaul failure.
UPF-NYC-01.impact: 340 PDU sessions dropped, QoS violations for voice and video

AMF-NYC-01.label: secondary_symptom
AMF-NYC-01.reasoning: Core network function affected by loss of NGAP connection to gNB-NYC-E-047. Registration failures and CPU spike from re-registration storm.
AMF-NYC-01.impact: NGAP connection lost, registration failure rate >20%, CPU spike

propagation_graph._comment: Directed graph showing how the fault propagated — the causal chain the GNN should learn

edges.mechanism: SFP failure on Gi0/0/0/3 → link down → CSG loses upstream OSPF adjacency
CSG upstream path lost → gNB loses S1/NG-C backhaul connectivity
CSG upstream path lost → eNB loses S1 backhaul connectivity
gNB SCTP/NGAP association lost → AMF detects connection loss
gNB S1-U/N3 path lost → UPF detects session drops, QoS violations
Overloaded neighbor — UEs from 047 attempt handover to 048, Xn handovers fail, interference increases
Traffic from CSG-NYC-E-01 rerouted via inter-AGG link → increased utilization on AGG-02
Rerouted traffic from site 047 passes through CSG-02's link to AGG-01 → congestion
OSPF reconvergence events from link flapping → excessive SPF calculations on core router

alarm_classification._comment: Each alarm classified as root_cause_indicator, symptom, or noise
root_cause_indicators: ALM-001: opticalRxPowerDegraded on AGG Gi0/0/0/3 — EARLIEST SIGNAL
ALM-003: interfaceCrcErrors (MAJOR, escalated) on AGG Gi0/0/0/3
direct_symptoms: ALM-005: linkDown on CSG-NYC-E-01 (first downstream alarm)
cascading_symptoms: ALM-013 to ALM-023: RAN cell unavailability (10 alarms)
ALM-024 to ALM-026: Core session/registration failures (3 alarms)
ALM-033 to ALM-034: Excessive UE releases (2 alarms)
ALM-038 to ALM-039: QoS violations (2 alarms)
collateral_noise: ALM-027 to ALM-028: highUtilization from rerouting (expected)
ALM-029 to ALM-032: gNB-048 degradation from overload (collateral)
ALM-035 to ALM-037: CPU utilization (expected during storm)
ALM-040 to ALM-047: Various secondary effects (noise)

training_target_formats._comment: How these labels are used for different GNN tasks

root_cause_detection.description: Binary classification: is this node the root cause? (0=no, 1=yes)

root_cause_ranking.description: Regression target: probability/score that each node is the root cause. Used for ranking.
